title: Arthrochilium handelii
taxonomy: Arthrochilium handelii is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Arthrochilium handelii is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. This species is endemic to Ecuador and is considered rare. Further classification details of Arthrochilium handelii may require more specific information.

intro: Arthrochilium handelii, also known as Handel's orchid, is a species of orchid that is native to the tropical regions of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Arthrochilium handelii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin-related disorders.
